subject: Request: Cloudflare Access auth for dashboard admin endpoints

body.request: Configure Cloudflare Access (Zero Trust) for the interagent compositor dashboard. Read-only endpoints remain public. Admin/lever endpoints require authenticated access.
body.rationale: The dashboard UX redesign introduces levers (autonomy budget adjustment, circuit breaker toggle, mode override, trigger sensitivity). These MUST NOT operate without auth. CF Access provides the auth layer with zero application-side auth flow code — CF handles login, issues JWT, worker verifies.

worker_side_changes.jwt_verification: Check CF-Access-JWT-Assertion header on /api/admin/* routes. Verify using CF Access public key. Reject with 403 if missing or invalid.
worker_side_changes.dashboard_detection: Add a /api/auth/status endpoint that returns {authenticated: true/false, identity: email}. Dashboard calls this on load to show/hide admin controls.
worker_side_changes.cors: No CORS changes needed — same-origin requests only for admin endpoints.

ops_responsibilities: Create CF Access application for interagent.safety-quotient.dev
Set policy: allow safety-quotient-lab org members
Apply path-level policy: /api/admin/* requires auth, everything else public
Share the CF Access public key or team domain for JWT verification in the worker
psychology_agent_responsibilities: Add JWT verification to worker.js for /api/admin/* routes
Add admin control visibility toggle in dashboard based on auth status
Implement the lever endpoints behind auth gate

claims.text: CF Access provides auth with zero application-side auth flow code. The CF edge handles login, session, and token issuance.
claims.confidence_basis: Cloudflare Access documentation and existing CF Workers integration pattern.

epistemic_flags: CF Access pricing: free for up to 50 users on the Free plan — should cover our team
GitHub OAuth as CF Access IdP requires CF to redirect to GitHub — verify this works with the existing CF tunnel setup
